purpose: Host-side daemons (and their jail-side counterparts) that give jails controlled access to host capabilities over unix sockets. Core pieces: host_service.py (generic framed-protocol server library), oauth_broker.py (host singleton that serializes Claude OAuth refreshes with a flock and owns the shared credentials file), broker_relay.py (per-jail byte proxy that dials the broker per connection and stamps jail_id), oauth_broker_jail.py (in-jail TLS terminator impersonating platform.claude.com), host_processes.py + yolo_ps.py (allowlisted host process viewer), and jail_daemon_supervisor.py (in-jail supervisor for loophole-declared daemons).

files.role: Library: unix-socket server framework (frame protocol v1: serve() + Session with stdout/stderr/json/exit/exec_allowlisted); also a smoke-test CLI via python -m src.host_service <sock>

Host daemon `yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host`: flock-serialized Claude OAuth refresh, background proactive refresher thread, HTTPS proxy to platform.claude.com, token-response mirroring into shared creds, CA/leaf cert generation (--init-ca), --self-check
Jail daemon: TLS terminator on 127.0.0.1:443 impersonating platform.claude.com; routes refresh grants to host broker (action=refresh), everything else via action=proxy

Per-jail host-side standalone process: raw byte proxy relay socket -> broker singleton, dials per connection, stamps jail_id into the first framed JSON message; stdlib-only by design (launched by absolute file path)
In-jail supervisor: parses YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS JSON env, spawns/restarts daemons per policy, per-daemon log rotation, signal-forwarding shutdown
Host daemon `yolo-host-processes`: ps-based allowlisted process viewer (modes list/tree/pid), config re-read per request from yolo-jail.jsonc, --self-check
Jail-side CLI `yolo-ps`: frames one JSON request over YOLO_SERVICE_HOST_PROCESSES_SOCKET, streams framed stdout/stderr, returns daemon exit code

public_surface.name: loophole frame protocol v1
public_surface.detail: Client sends exactly ONE request per connection: 4-byte big-endian uint32 length + UTF-8 JSON object. Server replies with frames: 1-byte stream_id (0=stdout,1=stderr,2=exit) + 4-byte BE uint32 length + payload. Exit frame payload = 4-byte BE int32 return code; connection ends after exit frame. Session.json emits newline-terminated JSON on stdout stream. Spec in docs/loophole-protocol.md. Unparseable/EOF-truncated request → server logs and closes with no frames. Handler exception → stderr frame 'handler error: <e>\n' + exit(1). Handler returning without exit → implicit exit(0).
public_surface.consumers: yolo_ps.py, oauth_broker_jail.ask_host_broker, broker_relay (partially protocol-aware), entrypoint journal client, cli/loopholes_runtime._broker_ping, any third-party loophole

public_surface.name: oauth broker request actions
public_surface.detail: JSON request field 'action' (default 'refresh'): {action:'refresh'} → {access_token,refresh_token,expires_in,token_type} or {error:'creds_unreadable'|'no_refresh_token'|'upstream_http'(+status,body[:200])|'upstream_unreachable'(+message)}; {action:'cached'} → same success shape or {error:'no_cached_token'}; {action:'proxy',method,path,headers:{},body_b64} → {status,headers,body_b64} or {error:'bad_request'|'bad_path'|'upstream_unreachable',message} (4xx/5xx upstream pass through verbatim in status); {action:'ping'} → {pong:true,pid:<int>}; unknown action → stderr 'unknown action: ...' + exit 2. 'jail_id' field stamped by relay used for access logging only.
public_surface.consumers: oauth_broker_jail.py (via relay), cli `yolo broker status`, `yolo doctor`

public_surface.name: host-processes request modes
public_surface.detail: {mode:'list'} → ps -o <fields> -C <comm>... output on stdout stream; {mode:'tree'} → filtered `ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args --forest` (allowlisted comms + direct children, header kept); {mode:'pid',pid:<int>} → ps -o <fields> -p <pid>, exit 2 if comm not allowlisted, exit 1 if pid missing; empty allowlist → stderr + exit 3; unknown mode → exit 2. exec_allowlisted timeout 30s → exit 124.

public_surface.name: broker relay behavior
public_surface.detail: Listens on <sockets_dir>/claude-oauth-broker.sock; per client connection: dials real broker socket fresh, reads first framed message (timeout 5s, max 4 MiB), JSON-parses, force-overwrites request['jail_id'] with container name, re-frames, then bidirectional dumb pipe. Non-JSON/oversize/slow first message → forwarded verbatim unstamped. Dial failure → SHUT_WR then bounded drain (5s) then close, so client sees clean EOF with zero frames (NOT ECONNRESET). SIGTERM/SIGINT → unlink own socket (only if dev/ino still matches what it bound) and exit 0.
public_surface.consumers: oauth_broker_jail.ask_host_broker error-layer attribution depends on these exact semantics (ENOENT/ECONNREFUSED=relay down; EOF-before-exit-frame=broker down; EPIPE/ECONNRESET/ENOTCONN mid-request=broker down)

public_surface.name: yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host
public_surface.detail: Entry point (pyproject [project.scripts] → src.oauth_broker:main). Flags: --socket PATH (required for daemon mode, else exit 2 with stderr message), --creds-file PATH (default ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/home/.claude-shared-credentials/.credentials.json), --init-ca / --force-init-ca (generate CA+leaf, print 'CA: <path>\nleaf: <path>', exit 0), --self-check (rc 0/1 with OK/NOTE/FAIL lines), --no-background-refresh, -v/--verbose.
public_surface.consumers: cli/loopholes_runtime._broker_ensure spawns it; manifest.jsonc host_daemon cmd; just deploy runs --init-ca; yolo doctor runs --self-check

public_surface.name: yolo-host-processes
public_surface.detail: Entry point → src.host_processes:main. Flags: --socket PATH (required unless --self-check; missing → stderr ERROR + exit 2), --config PATH (default $YOLO_HOST_PROCESSES_CONFIG or ./yolo-jail.jsonc), --self-check (prints OK/FAIL lines, rc 0/1).
public_surface.consumers: loopholes runtime spawns per manifest; yolo doctor

public_surface.name: yolo-ps
public_surface.detail: Entry point → src.yolo_ps:main (runs in jail). Flags: -t/--tree, --pid INT, --socket PATH override. No socket resolvable → stderr help text + exit 2; connect failure → stderr + exit 2. Exit code = daemon's exit frame (or 1 on truncated stream). Framed stdout/stderr copied byte-for-byte to local stdout/stderr.

public_surface.name: python3 -m src.oauth_broker_jail
public_surface.detail: Jail daemon (spawned by jail_daemon_supervisor per manifest). Flags: --host (127.0.0.1), --port (443), --cert/--key (default /var/lib/yolo-jail/loopholes/claude-oauth-broker/server.{crt,key}), --host-socket (default $YOLO_SERVICE_CLAUDE_OAUTH_BROKER_SOCKET), -v. Missing host socket env → exit 2; missing cert/key → exit 2. HTTP behavior: POST /v1/oauth/token with JSON body grant_type=refresh_token → broker refresh (200 with token JSON, 400 on broker {error}, 502 {error:'broker_unavailable',detail} on transport failure); all other methods/paths (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH) → proxied via action=proxy; 502 {error:'broker_bad_response'} on malformed broker reply. Default Content-Type application/json when upstream omits it; Content-Length recomputed.

public_surface.name: python3 <abs-path>/broker_relay.py
public_surface.detail: Args: --socket PATH (listen), --broker PATH (dial target), --jail NAME (jail_id stamp). All required. Launched by ABSOLUTE PATH (not -m) by loopholes_runtime._relay_ensure with start_new_session=True; stdout/stderr → ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/logs/broker-relay-<8hex>.log; PID written to /tmp/yolo-broker-relay-<8hex>.pid; discovered/reaped by `pgrep -f 'broker_relay.py --socket <path>'` and /proc/<pid>/cmdline containing 'broker_relay.py' — a Go replacement MUST keep 'broker_relay' + '--socket <path>' visible in argv or the supervision/reaping in loopholes_runtime breaks.

public_surface.name: python3 -m src.jail_daemon_supervisor
public_surface.detail: No flags. Reads YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS. Exit 0 when env unset/empty/no valid specs. Forked once by entrypoint (src/entrypoint/runtime.py start_jail_daemon_supervisor).

public_surface.name: YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS
public_surface.detail: JSON list of {name:str, cmd:[str...], restart:'always'|'on-failure'(default)|'no'} set by host-side loopholes loader (src/loopholes.py builds podman -e arg). Invalid JSON/non-list → logged, treated as empty. Restart backoff: 1s doubling to 30s cap.

public_surface.name: YOLO_SERVICE_CLAUDE_OAUTH_BROKER_SOCKET
public_surface.detail: In-jail path of the relay socket; naming rule YOLO_SERVICE_<sanitized upper name>_SOCKET (re.sub non-alnum → _). Read by oauth_broker_jail as --host-socket default.

public_surface.name: YOLO_SERVICE_HOST_PROCESSES_SOCKET
public_surface.detail: In-jail path of host-processes daemon socket; yolo-ps default. Missing → exit 2 with guidance.

public_surface.name: YOLO_JAIL_ID / HOSTNAME
public_surface.detail: yolo-ps sends jail_id from YOLO_JAIL_ID, falling back to HOSTNAME, else 'unknown' (note: relay-stamped jail_id overrides where a relay is in path; host-processes has no relay so client value is used for logging).

public_surface.name: YOLO_HOST_PROCESSES_CONFIG
public_surface.detail: Path to yolo-jail.jsonc for the host-processes daemon; also read by its self_check.

public_surface.name: ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/state/claude-oauth-broker/
public_surface.detail: Broker state dir (loopholes.state_dir_for): ca.crt, ca.key (0600), server.crt, server.key (0600), leaf.cnf, refresh.lock. CA: RSA-4096, x509 self-signed, 3650 days, subj /CN=yolo-jail-claude-oauth-broker/O=yolo-jail/OU=local. Leaf: RSA-2048, CN=platform.claude.com, SAN DNS:platform.claude.com,DNS:localhost, 3650 days, signed via openssl x509 -req -CAcreateserial.
public_surface.consumers: broker --init-ca writes; jail mount exposes leaf at /var/lib/yolo-jail/loopholes/claude-oauth-broker/; ca.crt referenced by manifest {state}/ca.crt for NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS

public_surface.name: shared credentials file
public_surface.detail: ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/home/.claude-shared-credentials/.credentials.json — {"claudeAiOauth": {accessToken, refreshToken, expiresAt(ms int), scopes[list], subscriptionType,...}} written json.dumps indent=2, atomically via mkstemp(prefix='.credentials.json.tmp.') + fchmod 0600 + os.replace in same dir. Cache-hit rule: valid iff expiresAt - now >= 90s. _normalize_oauth preserves prior keys, sets scopes from space-split response 'scope' only when absent and non-empty.
public_surface.consumers: Claude Code in every jail (directory bind-mount), entrypoint harvest, broker

public_surface.name: refresh.lock flock
public_surface.detail: open('w') + fcntl.flock LOCK_EX (blocking) around: cached-token check, upstream refresh, creds write. Same lock also taken by _maybe_propagate_token_response (proxy mirror path). Cross-PROCESS serialization of all refreshes host-wide.
public_surface.consumers: do_refresh, proxy mirror; multiple broker invocations

public_surface.name: upstream OAuth contract
public_surface.detail: POST https://platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token, JSON {grant_type:'refresh_token',refresh_token,client_id:'9d1c250a-e61b-44d9-88ed-5944d1962f5e'}, headers Content-Type: application/json, anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20, User-Agent: yolo-jail-oauth-broker/<pkg version> (Cloudflare 1010 blocks default UAs), 30s timeout. Proxy path strips hop-by-hop headers + content-length + accept-encoding (both directions; accept-encoding strip is load-bearing for the token mirror).

public_surface.name: host-service socket files
public_surface.detail: serve() binds socket under umask 0o077 then chmod 0600, unlinks pre-existing path before bind and unlinks on exit (unconditionally — no dev/ino guard, unlike relay). Broker singleton at /tmp/yolo-claude-oauth-broker.sock; per-jail sockets dir /tmp/yolo-host-services-<sha1(cname)[:8]>/ bind-mounted into jail; relay socket named claude-oauth-broker.sock inside it.

public_surface.name: in-jail daemon logs
public_surface.detail: ~/.local/state/yolo-jail-daemons/<name>.log, rotate-once to <name>.log.1 when >5 MB at daemon (re)start; child stdout+stderr both appended (open 'ab').

public_surface.name: SIGTERM/SIGINT handling
public_surface.detail: host_service.serve and broker_relay.serve: set stop flag, shutdown+close listener (macOS ENOTCONN quirk handled), unlink socket, return. jail_daemon_supervisor: SIGTERM/SIGINT → terminate() all children (SIGTERM, 5s wait, SIGKILL), join threads 10s, exit 0. Handlers only installed when on main thread.

public_surface.name: exit code conventions
public_surface.detail: 2 = usage/config error (missing --socket, missing env, allowlist violation, unknown action/mode, bad pid arg); 1 = runtime failure / handler exception / self-check FAIL / truncated stream in yolo-ps; 3 = host-processes empty allowlist; 124 = exec_allowlisted timeout; 0 success.

public_surface.name: host_processes (yolo-jail.jsonc)
public_surface.detail: {visible:[comm...], fields:[ps -o fields...]} — parsed with pyjson5; missing file/section or parse error → {visible:[], fields:DEFAULT_FIELDS}; defaults fields=[pid,comm,args,etime,%cpu,%mem,rss]. Re-read on EVERY request (no daemon restart needed after edits).

internal_deps: src/loopholes.py (state_dir_for — only import in oauth_broker besides host_service)
src/cli/loopholes_runtime.py (spawner/supervisor of broker + relay + host daemons; not imported by this group but tightly coupled to its argv/pidfile/socket contracts)
src/loopholes.py (builds YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS payload + manifests reference these binaries)

third_party_deps.go_equivalent: github.com/titanous/json5 or github.com/yosuke-furukawa/json5; must match pyjson5 leniency for existing configs

third_party_deps.pkg: openssl (external binary, not a pip dep)
third_party_deps.used_for: CA + leaf cert generation in oauth_broker (--init-ca), with 6 hardcoded fallback paths
third_party_deps.go_equivalent: crypto/x509 + crypto/rsa stdlib — eliminates the binary dependency entirely, but changes ca.srl/serial handling and cert byte-for-byte output (parity should compare cert FIELDS, not bytes)

third_party_deps.used_for: host_processes exec target; loopholes_runtime relay discovery
third_party_deps.go_equivalent: keep exec'ing ps for output parity; do NOT reimplement via /proc (output format is the contract)

side_effects: Rewrites the shared Claude credentials file atomically (mkstemp+rename, 0600) — every jail's login state
Takes blocking exclusive flock on state/claude-oauth-broker/refresh.lock
Outbound HTTPS to platform.claude.com (refresh + arbitrary proxied OAuth-endpoint requests)
Generates 10-year CA/leaf keypairs on disk (0600 keys) via openssl subprocess; writes leaf.cnf, ca.srl
Creates/chmods/unlinks unix socket files under /tmp; broker relay writes /tmp/yolo-broker-relay-<hash>.pid
jail_daemon_supervisor spawns arbitrary argv from env JSON, writes/rotates logs under ~/.local/state/yolo-jail-daemons/
host_processes execs ps against live host process table
Background refresher thread makes unsolicited upstream refreshes every 60s tick when within 300s of expiry (5s fast-retry x12 after transient failure)
Logging: broker logs token FINGERPRINTS (sha256[:8]) — never raw tokens; relay logs lengths only
python_specifics: try relative import / except ImportError fall back to `from src import ...` (script vs package duality) — irrelevant in Go but means the same file must work both as module and script today
importlib.metadata.version('yolo-jail') for the User-Agent version — Go needs ldflags-injected version; UA string format is observable upstream
fcntl.flock LOCK_EX blocking — Go: syscall.Flock; must remain compatible with any remaining Python flock holders during incremental port (same lock file, same flock semantics — OK cross-language)
tempfile.mkstemp + os.replace atomic write with prefix '.credentials.json.tmp.' — entrypoint harvest may rely on tmp-name pattern not colliding; keep unique-suffix behavior

threading daemon threads everywhere (per-connection handlers, bg refresher, relay pipes, supervisor); Go goroutines map cleanly but Python's daemon-thread 'dies with process' shutdown becomes explicit context cancellation

signal.signal only from main thread + PEP 475 EINTR auto-retry semantics; the macOS shutdown()-on-listener ENOTCONN workaround comments encode platform quirks Go's net.Listener.Close handles differently (Close unblocks Accept portably — simpler in Go)

http.server ThreadingHTTPServer + BaseHTTPRequestHandler subclass created via type() with class attr injection (oauth_broker_jail) — replace with net/http + tls.Config; watch header canonicalization: Python passes upstream header names through verbatim, Go's http.Header canonicalizes (MIMEHeader) — could alter proxied response headers observable by Claude Code
dict(self.headers) on http.client.HTTPMessage: duplicate headers collapse (last wins) — Go http.Header is multi-valued; parity requires collapsing

urllib hop-by-hop stripping is manual; Go's http.Client auto-decompresses gzip when Accept-Encoding unset — must keep stripping accept-encoding on the FORWARD and beware Transport transparently setting it (DisableCompression=true to match Python byte-for-byte body_b64)

Python json.dumps default separators (', ', ': ') vs Go json.Marshal (no spaces) — framed request/response bodies differ byte-wise; consumers parse JSON so semantically fine, but any test comparing raw bytes must compare parsed values; relay re-frames with Python json.dumps ordering (insertion order + jail_id appended last)
subprocess.Popen with file-object stdout/stderr redirection and close_fds; start_new_session=True (setsid) for the relay — Go: SysProcAttr{Setsid:true}

os.waitpid(WNOHANG)/ChildProcessError zombie-reap dance in loopholes_runtime expects the relay to be its direct child — if relay becomes a Go binary the spawn/reap side stays Python and only argv matters
BaseHTTPRequestHandler auto-adds Server/Date headers to jail terminator responses — Go net/http adds Date but not Server; likely unobserved by Claude but noteworthy
int(oauth.get('expiresAt', 0)) accepts float ms values from JSON; Go must handle number as float64→int64
struct.pack('>I')/('>i')/('>BI') framing — trivial binary.BigEndian, but note exit code is SIGNED int32

seams.name: broker_relay binary
seams.contract: argv: <bin> --socket <path> --broker <path> --jail <name>; must contain the substring 'broker_relay' and '--socket <path>' in its cmdline (pgrep -f and /proc cmdline matching in loopholes_runtime — either keep the name broker_relay.py-compatible or update _relay_pid_cmdline_matches/_relay_pgrep patterns in the same change); listens on --socket (unlink stale file first is done by spawner; relay also unlinks defensively), dials --broker per connection, stamps jail_id, clean-EOF-on-dial-failure semantics, SIGTERM → unlink-own-socket-iff-same-inode + exit 0, logs to stderr
seams.swap_mechanism: loopholes_runtime._relay_ensure builds the spawn argv — swap `[sys.executable, relay_script, ...]` for `[go_relay_path, ...]` behind an env var (e.g. YOLO_GO_RELAY=/path) or a settings flag; everything else (pidfile, locks, kill, jail side) unchanged. Ideal FIRST port: stdlib-only, ~300 lines, pure socket semantics.
seams.parity_test: tests/test_broker_relay.py already drives it as a subprocess-ish unit; add a harness that runs the same scenario suite against either binary: stamp injection, oversize/slow/non-JSON first message verbatim forwarding, dial-failure clean EOF (assert client gets EOF not ECONNRESET), SIGTERM socket unlink, successor-inode no-unlink

seams.name: yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host binary
seams.contract: CLI flags (--socket/--creds-file/--init-ca/--force-init-ca/--self-check/--no-background-refresh/-v), frame protocol v1 server on --socket, action set {refresh,cached,proxy,ping} with exact JSON response shapes, flock on state/claude-oauth-broker/refresh.lock, atomic creds writes, 90s cache headroom, 300s/60s/5s×12 bg refresher timing, cert files in state dir, self-check OK/NOTE/FAIL text + rc
seams.swap_mechanism: pyproject console-script name is what loopholes_runtime and manifest.jsonc invoke ('yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host'); install a Go binary earlier on PATH under the same name, or point manifest host_daemon.cmd at the Go path. flock is kernel-level so a Go broker and Python proxy-mirror path can even coexist during transition.
seams.parity_test: tests/test_oauth_broker.py (1345 lines) is the spec; port key cases to a black-box suite that talks the socket protocol to either implementation with a fake upstream (httptest server + /etc/hosts-free URL override needed: TOKEN_URL/UPSTREAM_HOST are constants — Go version should accept an override env/flag FOR TESTS ONLY); byte-compare creds files after refresh (JSON-normalize), verify flock exclusion with two concurrent refreshes

seams.name: yolo-host-processes binary
seams.contract: flags --socket/--config/--self-check; env YOLO_HOST_PROCESSES_CONFIG; frame server; modes list/tree/pid with exact ps argv construction, exit codes 0/1/2/3/124, config re-read per request, jsonc parsing
seams.swap_mechanism: same console-script-name swap as broker ('yolo-host-processes' in manifest host_daemon.cmd)
seams.parity_test: tests/test_host_processes.py cases run against both via the socket; compare ps output passthrough and exit codes; tree-mode filter logic (comm match + one-level-deep ppid chaining, order: matches then children appended) must match line-for-line

seams.name: yolo-ps client
seams.contract: env YOLO_SERVICE_HOST_PROCESSES_SOCKET, flags -t/--pid/--socket, request shapes {jail_id, mode[, pid]}, framed-response streaming to stdout/stderr, exit code mapping (2 on no socket/connect fail, 1 on truncated stream, else daemon rc)
seams.swap_mechanism: PATH shadowing inside the jail image ('yolo-ps' console script / Nix wrapper) — client and daemon are independently swappable since the socket protocol is the contract
seams.parity_test: run both clients against the same live Python daemon; diff stdout/stderr/rc for list/tree/pid/missing-socket cases

seams.name: oauth_broker_jail (in-jail TLS terminator)
seams.contract: HTTPS server on 127.0.0.1:443 with the state-dir leaf cert; refresh-grant detection (POST /v1/oauth/token + JSON body grant_type=refresh_token → action=refresh; everything else → action=proxy with base64 body); response status/header/body passthrough; 502 shapes {error:'broker_unavailable'|'broker_bad_response',detail}; 400 on broker {error}; layer-attribution error strings ('relay unreachable —' vs 'host broker unreachable through the relay')
seams.swap_mechanism: manifest.jsonc jail_daemon.cmd is ['python3','-m','src.oauth_broker_jail'] — change to the Go binary path baked into the Nix image (requires just load && just install; entrypoint/flake rebuild). Supervisor treats it as opaque argv.
seams.parity_test: tests/test_oauth_broker_jail.py against both: fake relay socket implementing the frame protocol; curl --cacert against :port for refresh, authorization_code, GET passthrough, relay-down (ENOENT), broker-down (EOF-no-exit-frame) → assert exact 502 JSON and log-layer attribution; watch Go header canonicalization on proxied response headers

seams.name: jail_daemon_supervisor
seams.contract: env YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS JSON schema, log dir/rotation (~/.local/state/yolo-jail-daemons/<name>.log, 5MB, .1 suffix), restart policies + 1s→30s doubling backoff, SIGTERM/SIGINT → terminate children (TERM,5s,KILL), rc 0 on empty env
seams.swap_mechanism: entrypoint/runtime.py start_jail_daemon_supervisor spawns [sys.executable,'-m','src.jail_daemon_supervisor'] — swap the argv for a Go binary in the Nix image (entrypoint change → image rebuild). Clean seam: only env-in, files-out.
seams.parity_test: tests/test_jail_daemon_supervisor.py scenarios black-box: crash-loop backoff timing (coarse), on-failure vs always vs no, log rotation at threshold, signal forwarding ordering

seams.name: host_service framework (Go library, not a binary)
seams.contract: frame protocol v1 (docs/loophole-protocol.md) + serve() socket lifecycle (0600, unlink-before-bind, unlink-on-exit) + access-log line format 'jail=%s keys=%s rc=%s elapsed_ms=%d bytes_out=%d'
seams.swap_mechanism: not independently swappable — it's a library; port it once as a Go package and reuse for broker + host_processes ports. Cross-language interop is already proven: broker_relay and yolo_ps speak the wire protocol without importing host_service.
seams.parity_test: tests/test_host_service.py protocol cases as a conformance suite runnable against any server implementation via the smoke handler (echo {ok:true})

existing: tests/test_oauth_broker_jail.py (319 ln — grant routing, layer attribution, proxy passthrough)
tests/test_broker_relay.py (561 ln — stamping, verbatim fallback, dial-failure EOF semantics, signal/unlink)
tests/test_host_service.py (258 ln — frame protocol, exec_allowlisted, error paths)
tests/test_host_processes.py (279 ln — modes, allowlist, config reload)
integration touchpoints in test_handoff_bugs.py, test_state_separation.py, test_doctor_inactive_loopholes.py, test_macos_paths.py, test_cli_*.py
gaps: No end-to-end test of real TLS termination with the generated leaf (cert generation tested only via openssl presence assumptions)
No cross-process flock contention test (two broker PIDs racing) — only in-process
Upstream URL is a hardcoded constant; no injection point means true refresh-path tests mock urllib rather than exercising HTTP — a Go port needs a test-only base-URL override to run black-box parity
yolo_ps client has thin direct coverage (mostly exercised indirectly)
No test pins the access-log line format or broker log fingerprint format (operators grep these)
Supervisor backoff timing and log rotation race untested
test_coverage.assessment: Strong unit coverage, mostly white-box (imports the module, monkeypatches urllib/subprocess). For the port, the highest-value work is converting the protocol-level cases into implementation-agnostic black-box suites that drive a socket/binary, since those run unchanged against Go.

porting_hazards: loopholes_runtime identifies/reaps the relay by argv substring 'broker_relay.py' (pgrep -f and /proc/cmdline) — a renamed Go binary silently breaks reaping (leaked relays) unless the matcher is updated in lockstep or the binary keeps a compatible cmdline

Relay dial-failure semantics are precise: SHUT_WR first, bounded drain of client bytes, then close — Linux AF_UNIX discards unread rx queue on close and surfaces ECONNRESET instead of EOF; a naive Go Close() breaks oauth_broker_jail's layer attribution
Go net/http canonicalizes header names and multi-values; Python passes proxied response header names verbatim and collapses duplicates (last wins) — parity on the proxy path needs deliberate handling
Go http.Transport auto-gzip: must set DisableCompression and continue stripping accept-encoding on forwards, or the token-mirror body parse regresses (repeat of the 2026-05-12 logout loop)
exit frame is SIGNED big-endian int32; stream lengths unsigned uint32 — easy sign mixup
JSON number handling: expiresAt may arrive as float; Python int() truncates — match

flock file is opened 'w' (truncating) each time — harmless for flock but Go should use O_CREATE|O_WRONLY + Flock on the same path so Python and Go instances still mutually exclude during incremental rollout

openssl subprocess with 6 hardcoded fallback paths and exact error text (SystemExit message includes PATH + searched list) — self-check/doctor output text is user-facing; decide whether Go uses crypto/x509 (recommended) and accept text drift, or keep exec parity

BaseHTTPRequestHandler quirks in the jail terminator: HTTP/1.0 default protocol_version (no keep-alive) — Claude Code reconnects per request today; Go's default keep-alive changes connection behavior (probably fine, but observable)

Console-script names (yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host, yolo-host-processes, yolo-ps) are the manifest/doctor contract — the swap must install identically-named executables or update manifest.jsonc + loopholes_runtime spawn argv together
Background refresher timing constants (300/60/5×12) and the transient-vs-permanent error classification (only 'upstream_unreachable' triggers fast retry) encode incident fixes — port exactly
Session.exit idempotence + implicit exit(0) after handler return + 'handler error: <e>\n' text are wire-observable
relay socket unlink-on-exit is guarded by dev/ino match (successor protection); host_service unlink is UNguarded — keep the asymmetry

macOS: _host_service_sockets_dir resolves /tmp→/private/tmp; AF_UNIX 104/108-byte path limits; ENOTCONN-vs-EPIPE send-after-close difference already encoded in oauth_broker_jail errno list — Go must map syscall errnos equivalently on darwin

In-jail components (oauth_broker_jail, jail_daemon_supervisor, yolo-ps) are baked into the Nix image — swapping them needs flake.nix packaging of Go binaries for the container arch + `just load && just install`, unlike host-side swaps which are live
notes: Port order recommendation: (1) broker_relay — stdlib-only, pure socket semantics, spawner-side argv swap, existing dedicated test file; (2) host_service as a Go library + conformance suite; (3) yolo-host-processes + yolo-ps as the first full daemon/client pair; (4) oauth_broker host daemon (highest risk: flock coexistence, upstream HTTP fidelity, creds-file atomicity — but fully host-side so live-swappable); (5) in-jail pieces last (image rebuild required). Wire protocol spec: docs/loophole-protocol.md; refresh mechanics: docs/claude-oauth-refresh-mechanics.md. Key files: /workspace/src/host_service.py, /workspace/src/oauth_broker.py, /workspace/src/broker_relay.py, /workspace/src/oauth_broker_jail.py, /workspace/src/host_processes.py, /workspace/src/yolo_ps.py, /workspace/src/jail_daemon_supervisor.py; coupling hot-spots: /workspace/src/cli/loopholes_runtime.py (spawn/reap contracts), /workspace/src/bundled_loopholes/*/manifest.jsonc (cmd names), /workspace/pyproject.toml lines 40-42 (console scripts), /workspace/src/entrypoint/runtime.py (supervisor fork).
